key_insight: A semi-automated in-situ imaging pipeline shows that MoS2 CVD growth is edge-attachment-limited and largely non-competitive until flakes physically impinge.
engineering_relevance: This provides quantitative guidance for tuning CVD conditions to improve reproducibility and control in scalable 2D TMD synthesis.
method: In-situ optical microscopy of MoS2 growth during CVD
Semi-automated image processing to reconstruct individual crystal trajectories
Statistical analysis across a large crystal ensemble
evidence_signal: The pipeline reconstructed complete growth trajectories for over 400 individual crystals from a single experiment, enabling statistical discrimination of the growth mechanism.
watch_item: The approach depends on clear in-situ imaging and robust tracking, so overlap, poor contrast, or more complex growth scenes may reduce automation accuracy.
paper_title: Revealing the MoS2 Growth Mechanism in Chemical Vapor Deposition: Real-Time Imaging and Statistical Analysis
